# Builtin golden corpus.
# Expected shapes were checked against GNU coreutils/bash behavior where the
# supported feature is a GNU-compatible surface. macOS BSD-compatible cases
# were also spot-checked locally. Known documented deviations: grep and sed use
# Rust regex syntax rather than GNU BRE/ERE syntax for linear-time matching.

CASE cat stdin dash
COMMAND
echo -e 'a\nb' | cat -
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
a
b
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE ls all long shape
COMMAND
mkdir -p /d; touch /d/.hidden /d/file; ls -a -l /d
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
drwxr-xr-x 1 0 0      0 Jan  1 00:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 0 0      0 Jan  1 00:00 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 0 0      0 Jan  1 00:00 .hidden
-rw-r--r-- 1 0 0      0 Jan  1 00:00 file
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE cp mv rm mkdir touch
COMMAND
mkdir -p /a; echo data > /a/src; cp /a/src /a/copy; mkdir /b; mv /a/copy /b/moved; rm /a/src; ls /a /b
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
/a:

/b:
moved
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE echo clusters literals and escapes
COMMAND
echo -nnee 'x\ny'; echo -- -x
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
x
y-- -x
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE pwd after cd
COMMAND
mkdir -p /work; cd /work; pwd
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
/work
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE grep flags and no match
COMMAND
echo -e 'Alpha\nbeta\nALPHA' > /g; grep -i -n alpha /g; grep -c -v beta /g; grep zzz /g
END_COMMAND
EXIT 1
STDOUT
1:Alpha
3:ALPHA
2
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE grep read error exits two
COMMAND
echo ok > /g; grep ok /g /missing
END_COMMAND
EXIT 2
STDOUT
/g:ok
END_STDOUT
STDERR
grep: /missing: No such file or directory
END_STDERR
END

CASE grep recursive prefixes paths
COMMAND
mkdir -p /r; echo one > /r/a; echo two > /r/b; grep -r o /r
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
/r/a:one
/r/b:two
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE head tail headers and plus count
COMMAND
echo -e 'a\nb\nc' > /h1; echo -e 'd\ne\nf' > /h2; head -n 1 /h1 /h2; tail -n +2 /h1
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
==> /h1 <==
a

==> /h2 <==
d
b
c
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE head rejects plus count
COMMAND
echo -e 'a\nb' | head -n +1
END_COMMAND
EXIT 1
STDOUT
END_STDOUT
STDERR
head: invalid number of lines: '+1'
END_STDERR
END

CASE sort numeric reverse unique
COMMAND
echo -e '10abc\n2\nx\n-3.5z\n2' | sort -n -u; echo -e 'a\nc\nb' | sort -r
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
-3.5z
x
2
10abc
c
b
a
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE sort numeric unique dedups by key
COMMAND
echo -e '10\n10abc' | sort -n -u
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
10
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE uniq count repeated unique
COMMAND
echo -e 'a\na\nb\nc\nc' > /u; uniq -c /u; uniq -d /u; uniq -u /u
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
      2 a
      1 b
      2 c
a
c
b
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE wc width rules
COMMAND
echo -e -n 'one two\n' | wc; echo -e 'a b\nc' > /w; wc -l /w; wc -l -w -c /w /w
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
      1       2       8
2 /w
 2  3  6 /w
 2  3  6 /w
 4  6 12 total
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE sed per line replacement rules
COMMAND
echo -e 'a\na\nprice\naba' | sed 's/a/b/'; echo price | sed 's/price/$100/'; echo ab | sed 's/(a)(b)/\2\1&/'; echo aa | sed 's/a/X/g'
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
b
b
price
bba
$100
baab
XX
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE sed replacement newline
COMMAND
echo a | sed 's/a/1\n2/'
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
1
2
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE cat numbering squeeze and marks
COMMAND
echo -e 'a\tx\n\n\n\nb' > /f; cat -n /f; cat -b /f; cat -sn /f; cat -ET /f
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
     1	a	x
     2	
     3	
     4	
     5	b
     1	a	x



     2	b
     1	a	x
     2	
     3	b
a^Ix$
$
$
$
b$
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE cat numbering continues across files
COMMAND
echo -e 'p\nq' > /g; cat -n /g /g
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
     1	p
     2	q
     3	p
     4	q
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END

CASE cat unsupported flag
COMMAND
cat -z /g
END_COMMAND
EXIT 1
STDOUT
END_STDOUT
STDERR
cat: unsupported option '-z'
END_STDERR
END

CASE sed invalid replacement reference
COMMAND
echo x | sed 's/x/\1/'
END_COMMAND
EXIT 1
STDOUT
END_STDOUT
STDERR
sed: invalid reference \1 on `s' command's RHS
END_STDERR
END

CASE sed usage error exits one
COMMAND
sed y
END_COMMAND
EXIT 1
STDOUT
END_STDOUT
STDERR
sed: unsupported command; thinbox supports s/// with g and i flags
END_STDERR
END

CASE which and stat
COMMAND
which cat; stat /bin/cat
END_COMMAND
EXIT 0
STDOUT
/bin/cat
  File: /bin/cat
  Size: 0
  Type: regular file
END_STDOUT
STDERR
END_STDERR
END
